title: Molten Festive Syrian Fennel Tagine
description: A pungent and festive Syrian dish featuring fennel bulb, mantecare emulsification, and nut-free friendly ingredients, cooked in a banana leaf vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine chopped fennel, garlic, lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the blended fennel mixture to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is heated through and starting to emulsify.
Lay a banana leaf flat on a work surface and spoon the fennel mixture onto the center of the leaf.
Fold the banana leaf over the filling to form a tight package, and repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Place the packages in a large baking dish and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the packages from the oven and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the molten tagine hot, garnished with additional chopped fennel fronds if desired.
